creator: bofh@freebsd.org
text:
I am closing this ticket for the following reasons:
1. At this moment we are trying to drop support(ports tree will be there but no support here) for ports tree for the end users as it's not possible to reproduce the exact environment(which ports are installed which are not installed and what other OPTIONS are enabled/disabled whatt DEFAUL_VERSIONS for what application are used) of an end user. From our experience in most cases it's for some misconfiguration or broken/unclean git tree from portsnap at user end which eventually causes priceless time for a developer/committer. If the end user is not using pkg or poudriere build system to build their own pkgs then they should mail the relevant mailing lists or dive in IRC to get help.

2. This just builds fine. Although not needed but I just triggered a rebuild of this port for 124i386 and 140. See for yourself:
https://pkg.bofh.network/data/latest-per-pkg/php81-opcache/8.1.25/

And there is also no build failure in portsfallout:

https://portsfallout.com/port?port=www%2Fphp81-opcache&maintainer=
